Name: cuda_kernel_opt
Title: Optimizing CUDA Kernels for Efficient Inference on Commodity GPUs
Short Hypothesis: By optimizing CUDA kernels for efficient inference on commodity GPUs, we can achieve significant end-to-end speedups and improve the overall performance of machine learning models.
Related Work: Recent works such as Spectral Latent Steering have focused on test-time optimization and latency-aware frameworks, but have not directly addressed the optimization of CUDA kernels for efficient inference. Our proposal builds upon existing research in this area and aims to provide a more comprehensive and hardware-faithful approach to optimizing CUDA kernels.
Abstract: This proposal presents a novel approach to optimizing CUDA kernels for efficient inference on commodity GPUs. By leveraging a combination of kernel fusion, memory-bandwidth utilization, KV-cache layout, and tensor-core occupancy optimization, we aim to achieve significant end-to-end speedups and improve the overall performance of machine learning models. Our approach is grounded in a thorough understanding of the underlying hardware and is designed to provide reproducible and deployable results.
Experiments: Optimizing CUDA kernels for a range of machine learning models, including convolutional neural networks and recurrent neural networks
Evaluating the performance of optimized kernels using metrics such as wall-clock latency, tokens-per-second, and energy per token
Comparing the performance of optimized kernels against baseline kernels and other state-of-the-art optimization techniques
Investigating the impact of kernel fusion, memory-bandwidth utilization, KV-cache layout, and tensor-core occupancy on kernel performance
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of optimizing CUDA kernels for efficient inference may be higher than anticipated, requiring significant development and testing time
The performance gains achieved through kernel optimization may be limited by other bottlenecks in the system, such as memory bandwidth or storage constraints
The optimized kernels may not be compatible with all commodity GPUs, requiring additional development and testing to ensure broad compatibility
